---
title: "2.2 Boolean Expressions"
subtitle: "Selection and Iteration"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit2-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Boolean Results

### Conditions produce `true` or `false`

A <span class="term">Boolean expression</span> is a test with only two possible results:

::: {.tight-list}
- `true`
- `false`
:::

These results drive selection statements later in the unit.

## Equality Testing

### `==` tests, `=` assigns

```java
int age = 15;
int year = 14;
System.out.println(age == year);
System.out.println(age != year);
```

::: {.tight-list}
- one `=` changes a variable
- two `==` compare values
- `!=` means not equal
:::

This is one of the first branch-killing syntax mistakes students make.

## Reference Equality

### With objects, `==` checks whether two references point to the same object

![Turtle reference equality](../../assets/2-selection-and-iteration/2-2-boolean-expressions/turtleEquality.png){fig-align="center" width="60%"}

::: {.tight-list}
- `juan == mia` is false when they are different turtles
- `friend == mia` is true when both references point to the same turtle
- this is <span class="mark">reference equality</span>, not content equality
:::

## Relational Operators

### Numeric comparisons also return Booleans

::: {.tight-list}
- `<` less than
- `>` greater than
- `<=` less than or equal to
- `>=` greater than or equal to
- `==` equals
- `!=` not equal
:::

```java
number > 0
number < 0
age <= year
```

## Testing with `%`

### Remainders are useful for even, odd, and divisibility tests

```java
number % 2 == 0
number % 2 != 0
number % x == 0
```

::: {.tight-list}
- even: remainder 0 when divided by 2
- odd: remainder not 0 when divided by 2
- divisible by `x`: remainder 0 when divided by `x`
:::

This is the bridge to prime-number and divisibility tasks.

## Classroom Tasks

### Practice worth keeping

Retained classroom work for this topic:

::: {.tight-list}
- truth-value checks on equality and relational operators
- reference-equality comparisons with turtle objects
- even / odd / divisible-by-`x` tests using `%`
- <span class="term">2.2.4 Coding Challenge: Prime Numbers POGIL</span>
:::

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- define a Boolean expression as a test that returns `true` or `false`
- distinguish `=` from `==`
- evaluate relational operators correctly
- explain `%` tests for even, odd, and divisibility
- recognize that `==` on objects checks references, not matching content
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
